case_metadata.title: Supply-Chain Entry → AD Certificate Services Abuse → Lateral Movement
case_metadata.scenario_class: The 'HOW DID THEY GET DOMAIN ADMIN' investigation — trojanized vendor binary + ESC8 + DCSync + Golden Ticket
case_metadata.ground_truth_provenance: Synthetic scenario; all hosts (MGMT-VELO-01, DC01, CA01, FS-DATA-02, WS-FIN-09), IPs (10.50.0.0/16 RFC1918, 198.51.100.0/24 + 203.0.113.0/24 RFC5737 documentation ranges) and domain (ent.example.local — RFC2606 reserved 'example' TLD) are deterministically generated and have no cross-reference to any real environment. Attack chain composed entirely from public references: SolarWinds SUNBURST (CISA AA20-352A, 2020-12); APT29 / Cozy Bear TTP (Mandiant white paper, 2020); ESC8 NTLM-relay-to-ADCS (SpecterOps 'Certified Pre-Owned', 2021); PetitPotam MS-EFSRPC coercion (CVE-2021-36942); BloodHound attack-path mapping; AdminSDHolder ACL abuse (Microsoft KB 232199); Golden Ticket TTP (MITRE T1558.001).
case_metadata.differentiation_from_case_05: case-05 entry vector is stolen-credential brute force + Kerberoasting RC4 TGS abuse. case-11 entry vector is supply-chain trojanized signed binary + ADCS template misconfiguration (ESC8) + PKINIT-based DA impersonation. No shared host, IP, account, timestamp, or domain.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Trojanized signed vendor binary executed on MGMT-VELO-01 — VeloLink-Agent.exe (PID 4012) parent-of rundll32 loading VeloLink.SecureCore.dll
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Vendor management binary as direct parent of rundll32 loading a custom DLL = abnormal vendor-binary behaviour. SolarWinds SUNBURST pattern (CISA AA20-352A): trojanized signed update payload.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Low-frequency C2 beacons from MGMT-VELO-01 (10.50.10.40) to 198.51.100.62 (telemetry-velolink-cdn.com) — ~8 min cadence, then ~5 MB exfil to 203.0.113.74
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Beacon cadence + large post-DCSync outbound transfer = staged exfil of credential material. APT29-style TTP.

ground_truth_findings.claim: PetitPotam coercion — PetitPotam.exe and ntlmrelayx.py executed back-to-back on MGMT-VELO-01, forcing DC01$ NTLM auth to CA01 certsrv endpoint
ground_truth_findings.rationale: PetitPotam (CVE-2021-36942 MS-EFSRPC) + ntlmrelayx --adcs = canonical ESC8 chain. The forced auth event also shows as 4624 type-3 from MGMT-VELO-01 → CA01 with DC01$ subject.

ground_truth_findings.claim: ADCS abuse (ESC8) — relayed NTLM authentication issued certificate to DC01$ via DomainController template, cached as dc01.pfx
ground_truth_findings.rationale: ntlmrelayx --adcs --template DomainController + subsequent Rubeus asktgt /certificate:dc01.pfx = textbook ESC8 (SpecterOps 'Certified Pre-Owned'). ADCS 4886/4887 audit events were NOT centralized — detection-gap callout.

ground_truth_findings.claim: PKINIT TGT request for DC01$ from anomalous source IP 10.50.10.40 (MGMT-VELO-01) — DC should authenticate from itself (10.50.10.10)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: EventID 4768 with PreAuthType=16 (PKINIT) + TicketEncryptionType 0x12 (AES256, cert-based) from a non-DC source IP. Repeated 4 times across 3 minutes from 4 different sources → users_with_scattered_tgts trigger.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Type-9 NewCredentials logon on DC01 — Subject DC01$ impersonating domadmin via Kerberos PKINIT (S4U2self abuse)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Logon type 9 (NewCredentials, RunAs /netonly equivalent) appearing on a DC with Subject=DC01$ is highly anomalous. Combined with PKINIT auth package = S4U2self → DA impersonation. Rubeus s4u command in process tree confirms.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Lateral movement — psexec to DC01, psexec to FS-DATA-02, wmiexec.py with overpass-the-hash to WS-FIN-09 (all from MGMT-VELO-01)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Three remote-admin tool invocations within 90 seconds, all from same source host, targeting different categories (DC / file server / endpoint). wmiexec with -hashes flag = overpass-the-hash via NTLM.

ground_truth_findings.claim: DCSync — mimikatz lsadump::dcsync /user:krbtgt extracted KRBTGT credential from DC01
ground_truth_findings.rationale: lsadump::dcsync targeting krbtgt = canonical DCSync attack. The KRBTGT hash is the master key for forging Kerberos tickets domain-wide; this is the highest-impact credential theft possible against AD.

ground_truth_findings.claim: AD database extraction — ntdsutil 'ifm create full' dumped entire NTDS.dit to c:\temp\ntds
ground_truth_findings.rationale: ntdsutil ac in ntds ifm create full = official Microsoft tool abused to copy the entire AD database including all account hashes. Combined with the DCSync above = belt-and-suspenders credential theft.

ground_truth_findings.claim: AdminSDHolder ACL modified — dsacls.exe granted ENT\svc-velolink GenericAll over AdminSDHolder container
ground_truth_findings.rationale: AdminSDHolder is the ACL template re-applied every 60 minutes (SDProp) to every protected admin group (Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, etc.). Granting an attacker account GenericAll on AdminSDHolder = automatic, self-healing privileged persistence. Removal of one access grant won't kick the attacker out.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Golden Ticket indicator — 4768 for krbtgt user with PreAuthType=0 and RC4 encryption (no preceding 4624)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: krbtgt account does not authenticate; its TGT requests should never appear with PreAuth=0. The 4768 for TargetUserName=krbtgt with RC4 encryption type is a known forged-TGT artefact (AS-REP-roasting detector will also flag it as a side-effect via the PreAuthType=0 condition).

ground_truth_findings.claim: Security/System/Application log clearing on MGMT-VELO-01 (EventID 1102 + wevtutil cl)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Three sequential wevtutil cl invocations + EventID 1102 self-emission = explicit log destruction. Performed only on MGMT-VELO-01 (initial-access host) so DC traces survive — operator-error giveaway that aids attribution to the supply-chain entry point.
